questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $-\frac{\pi}{2} \lt \alpha_1 \lt \alpha_2 \lt \alpha_3 \lt \frac{\pi}{2}$, then number of values of $\theta \in\left(-\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$ satisfying $\left(\tan \theta-\tan \alpha_1\right)\left(\tan \theta-\tan \alpha_2\right)\left(\tan \theta-\tan \alpha_3\right)-\left(\sum\left(\tan \theta-\tan \alpha_i\right)\right)$ is ______ .
questions.solution:
Let $f(\theta)=\Pi\left(\tan \theta-\tan \alpha_i\right)-\left(\sum\left(\tan \theta-\tan \alpha_i\right)\right)$
We have $f(\theta)_{\theta \rightarrow-\pi / 2} \rightarrow-\infty$
Again $\mathrm{f}\left(\alpha_1\right)\gt0, \mathrm{f}\left(\alpha_3\right) \lt 0$ and $\mathrm{f}(\theta)_{\theta \rightarrow \pi / 2} \rightarrow \infty$
$\Rightarrow$ There will be 3 such values of ' $\theta$ '
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Number of solution of the equation $\sin ^4 x-\cos ^2 x \sin x+2 \sin ^2 x+\sin x=0$ in $0 \leq x \leq 3 \pi$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \sin ^2 x\left[\sin ^2 x+\sin x+2\right]=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \sin x=0, \text { where } x=0, \pi, 2 \pi, 3 \pi\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the largest possible value of $x$ such that $0 < x < \pi$ satisfying the equation $\frac{2 \sin 2 x+1}{\cos x+\sin x}=\sqrt{2}$ is $\frac{a \pi}{b}$. Find $(a+b)$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} 2\left((\cos x+\sin x)^2-1\right) & =\sqrt{2}(\cos x+\sin x) \\ C+S & =t \\ 2 t^2-1 & =\sqrt{2} t \Rightarrow 2 t^2-\sqrt{2} t-1=0 \\ t & =\frac{\sqrt{2} \pm \sqrt{2+8}}{4}=\frac{\sqrt{2} \pm \sqrt{10}}{4} \\ \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(\cos x+\sin x) & =\frac{\sqrt{2}(1-\sqrt{5})}{4} \\ \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & =-\left(\frac{\sqrt{5}-1}{4}\right) \\ x+\frac{\pi}{4} & =\frac{-\pi}{10} \text { or } \frac{11 \pi}{10} \\ x & =\frac{11 \pi}{10}-\frac{\pi}{4}=\frac{17 \pi}{20} \equiv \frac{a \pi}{b} \Rightarrow a+b=37\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If sum of the roots of the equation
$$
\sqrt{2} \sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\left(\sec ^2 x-(1+\sqrt{3}) \tan x+2+\sqrt{3}\right)=\sqrt{9+18 \sin x \cdot \cos x} \text { in }[0,2 \pi] \text { is } \frac{p}{q} \pi
$$
where $p, q \in N$, then find the least value of $|p-q|$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & (25)(\sin x+\cos x)\left(\tan ^2 x-(1+\sqrt{3}) \tan x+3+\sqrt{3}\right)=3|\sin x+\cos x| \\ & \text { Case-1: } \sin x+\cos x \geq 0 \\ & (\sin x+\cos x)\left(\tan ^2 x-(1+\sqrt{3}) \tan x+\sqrt{3}\right)=0 \\ & \tan x=-1,1, \sqrt{3} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{3 \pi}{4}, \frac{\pi}{4}, \frac{\pi}{3}, \frac{7 \pi}{4} \\ & \text { Case-2: } \sin x+\cos x < 0 \\ & (\sin x+\cos x) \underbrace{\left(\tan ^2 x-(1+\sqrt{3}) \tan x+6+\sqrt{3}\right)}_{\text {no solution }}=0 \\ & \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \tan x=-1 \Rightarrow x=\frac{3 \pi}{4}, \frac{7 \pi}{4} \\ & \therefore \text { Required sum }=\frac{37 \pi}{12}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The number of integral values of $a ; a \in(6,100)$ for which the equation $[\tan x]^2+\tan x-a=0$ has real roots; where [.] denotes greatest integer function is __________ .
questions.solution:
$[\tan \mathrm{x}]^2+\tan \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{a}=0$
$\because[\tan \mathrm{x}]^2$ and a are integral in nature $\Rightarrow \tan \mathrm{x}$ is integer.
$\Rightarrow$ equation reduces to $\tan ^2 \mathrm{x}+\tan \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{a}=0$
$\Rightarrow \tan \mathrm{x}(\tan \mathrm{x}+1)=\mathrm{a}$
$\therefore$ a is product of consecutive integer
Here $\mathrm{a}=12,20,30,42,56,72,90$
$\Rightarrow$ a has 7 values.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let maximum value of the expression $y=|k-3| \cos 2 x+|t-4| \sin 2 x+3$ where $0 \leq k \leq 6$ and $1 \leq t \leq 7$ is equal to 6 then find the minimum value of $\left(k^2+t^2\right)$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
\left.y\right|_{\max .} & =\sqrt{(k-3)^2+(t-4)^2}+3=6 \\
(k-3)^2+(t-4)^2 & =9
\end{aligned}
$$

Put
$$
k-3=3 \cos \theta
$$

Put
$$
t+4=3 \sin \theta
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
k^2+t^2 & =(3+3 \cos \theta)^2+(-4+3 \sin \theta)^2 \\
& =34+18 \cos \theta-24 \sin \theta \\
+\left.t^2\right|_{\min .} & =(34-30)=4
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\therefore \quad k^2+\left.t^2\right|_{\min .}=(34-30)=4
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\cot (\theta-\alpha), 3 \cot \theta, \cot (\theta+\alpha)$ are in A.P. and $\theta$ is not an integral multiple of $\frac{\pi}{2}$, then find the value of $\frac{2 \sin ^2 \theta}{\sin ^2 \alpha}$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} 6 \cot \theta & =\cot (\theta-\alpha)+\cot (\theta+\alpha) \\ 6 \quad 6 \cot \theta & =\frac{\cos (\theta-\alpha)}{\sin (\theta-\alpha)}+\frac{\cos (\theta+\alpha)}{\sin (\theta+\alpha)} \\ 6 \cot \theta & =\frac{\sin 2 \theta}{\sin ^2 \theta-\sin ^2 \alpha}=\frac{2 \sin \theta \cos \theta}{\sin ^2 \theta-\sin ^2 \alpha} \\ \frac{3}{\sin \theta} & =\frac{\sin \theta}{\sin ^2 \theta-\sin ^2 \alpha} \\ 3 \sin ^2 \theta-3 \sin ^2 \alpha & =\sin ^2 \theta \Rightarrow \frac{2 \sin ^2 \theta}{\sin ^2 \alpha}=3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Given $A=\cos ^2 \theta\left(\cos ^2 \theta+1\right)+2 \sin ^2 \theta$. Then for all $\theta,[A]$ can assume values whose sum is ______ (Where [.] denotes the greatest integer function).
questions.solution: $\text { On solving we have } A=\cos ^4 \theta+1-\cos ^2 \theta+1=\left(\cos ^2 \theta-\frac{1}{2}\right)^2+\frac{7}{4} \Rightarrow A \in\left[\frac{7}{4}, 2\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If $x, y$ and $z$ are real numbers that satisfy the three equations
$$
\left\{\begin{array}{l}
\tan (x)+\tan (y)+\tan (z)=6-(\cot (x)+\cot (y)+\cot (z)) \\
\tan ^2(x)+\tan ^2(y)+\tan ^2(z)=6-\left(\cot ^2(x)+\cot ^2(y)+\cot ^2(z)\right) \\
\tan ^3(x)+\tan ^3(y)+\tan ^3(z)=6-\left(\cot ^3(x)+\cot ^3(y)+\cot ^3(z)\right)
\end{array}\right.
$$

Find the value of the expression $\left(\frac{\tan (x)}{\tan (y)}+\frac{\tan (y)}{\tan (z)}+\frac{\tan (z)}{\tan (x)}+3 \tan (x) \tan (y) \tan (z)\right)$.
questions.solution:
Let $\tan x=a, \tan y=b, \tan z=c$
Given system of equation is equal to
$$
\begin{aligned}
a+b+c & =6-\frac{1}{a}-\frac{1}{b}-\frac{1}{c} \\
a^2+b^2+c^2 & =6-\frac{1}{a^2}-\frac{1}{b^2}-\frac{1}{c^2} \\
a^3+b^3+c^3 & =6-\frac{1}{a^3}-\frac{1}{b^3}-\frac{1}{c^3}
\end{aligned}
$$

From second equation we complete squares to get
$$
\left(a-\frac{1}{a}\right)^2+\left(b-\frac{1}{b}\right)^2+\left(c-\frac{1}{c}\right)^2=0 \Rightarrow a=b=c= \pm 1
$$

Now rearranging $3^{\text {rd }}$ equation and adding 3 time first equation, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
a^3+b^3+c^3+\frac{1}{a^2}+\frac{1}{b^2}+\frac{1}{c^2}+3\left(a+\frac{1}{a}+b+\frac{1}{b}+c+\frac{1}{c}\right) & =6+18 \\
\left(a+\frac{1}{a}\right)^3+\left(b+\frac{1}{b}\right)^3+\left(c+\frac{1}{c}\right)^3 & =24
\end{aligned}
$$

Therefore $a=b=c=1$
Hence, $\left[\frac{\tan (x)}{\tan (y)}+\frac{\tan (y)}{\tan (z)}+\frac{\tan (z)}{\tan (x)}+3 \tan (x) \tan (y) \tan (z)\right]=6$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $0 < \theta < \frac{\pi}{2}$, such that $\frac{\sin ^2 2 \theta+4 \sin ^4 \theta-4 \sin ^2 \theta \cos ^2 \theta}{4-4 \sin ^2 \theta-\sin ^2 2 \theta}=\frac{7-4 \sqrt{3}}{7+4 \sqrt{3}}$, then $\theta=\frac{\pi}{n}$ where $n$ is:
questions.solution: $\begin{array}{rlrl} & \frac{4 \sin ^4 \theta}{4 \cos ^2 \theta-4 \sin ^2 \theta \cdot \cos ^2 \theta} & =\frac{\sin ^4 \theta}{\cos ^2 \theta \cdot \cos ^2 \theta}=(\tan \theta)^4 \\ & \therefore & (\tan \theta)^4 & =\left(\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{2+\sqrt{3}}\right)^2=(2-\sqrt{3})^4 \\ & \therefore & \tan \theta & =2-\sqrt{3} \Rightarrow \theta=\frac{\pi}{12}\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\left(\frac{1}{4} \cos 36^{\circ} \sin 54^{\circ}\right)^2-\left(\sin 12^{\circ} \sin 36^{\circ} \sin 48^{\circ} \sin 72^{\circ}\right)^2=\frac{\sqrt{a}-b}{c}$ where $a, c$ are relatively prime numbers, then find the value of $(a+b+c)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{16}\left(\cos 36^{\circ} \sin 54^{\circ}\right)^2-\left(\frac{1}{4} \sin 36^{\circ} \sin 36^{\circ}\right)^2 \\ & \frac{1}{16}\left(\cos ^2 36^{\circ}-\sin ^2 36^{\circ}\right)=\frac{1}{16} \cos 72^{\circ}=\frac{\sqrt{5}-1}{64} \equiv \frac{\sqrt{a}-b}{c} \\ & \therefore(a+b+c)=5+1+64=70\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $0 \leq \theta \leq 2 \pi$ and $x=|\cos \theta+1|+|\cos \theta-1|+|\cos \theta-2|+|\cos \theta-3|$, then product of the maximum and minimum values of $x$ is:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} x & =(\cos \theta+1)-(\cos \theta-1)-(\cos \theta-2)-(\cos \theta-3) \\ \therefore \quad x & =7-2 \cos \theta \\ x_{\text {max }} & =7-2(-1)=9 \\ x_{\text {min }} & =7-2(1)=5\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The number of real solutions of the equation $\sqrt{1+\cos 2 x}=\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}(\sin x)$ where $-\pi \leq x \leq \pi$.
questions.solution: $$ \begin{aligned} & & \sqrt{1+\cos 2 x} & =\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}(\sin x) \\ \Rightarrow & & \sqrt{2}|\cos x| & =\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}(\sin x) \\ \Rightarrow & & |\cos x| & =\sin ^{-1}(\sin x) \end{aligned} $$ When we draw the graph both functions (shown below) we can actually see that they intersect only at two points $\forall x \in-\pi \leq x \leq \pi$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$$
a^2 \cot 9^{\circ}+b^2 \cot 27^{\circ}+c^2 \cot 63^{\circ}+d^2 \cot 81^{\circ}
$$
Let $a, b, c$ and $d$ be real numbers satisfying $a+b+c+d=5$. If the minimum value of the expression above is equal to $\frac{\sqrt{x}}{y}$, where $x$ and $y$ are co-prime positive integers, find $x+y$
questions.solution:
$$
\left(a^2 \cot 9^{\circ}+d^2 \tan 9^{\circ}\right)+\left(b^2 \cot 27^{\circ}+c^2 \tan 27^{\circ}\right) ...(1)
$$
Given $a+b+d=5$
Let $\quad a+d=k_1$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad b+c & =k_2 \\
\text { Let } \quad k_1+k_2 & =5 \\
E_1 & =a^2 \cot 9^{\circ}+d^2 \tan 9^{\circ} \\
E_1 & =a^2 \cot 9^{\circ}+\left(k_1-a\right)^2 \tan 9^{\circ} ...(2)\\
E_1 & =a^2\left(\cot 9^{\circ}+\tan 9^{\circ}\right)-a k_1 \tan 9^{\circ}+k_1^2 \tan 9^{\circ}
\end{aligned}
$$
This is quadratic in $a$ whose minimum value is obtained at $x=\frac{-B}{2 A}$
i.e.,
$$
a=\frac{2 k_1 \tan 9^{\circ}}{2\left(\cot 9^{\circ}+\tan 9^{\circ}\right)}=k_1 \sin ^2 9^{\circ}
$$

Let
$$
\begin{aligned}
E_2 & =b^2 \cot 27^{\circ}+c^2 \tan 27^{\circ} \\
b+c & =k_2 \\
E_2 & =b^2 \cot 27^{\circ}+\left(k^2-b\right)^2 \tan 27^{\circ} ...(3)
\end{aligned}
$$

Make quadratic in $b$
$E_2 \mathrm{~min}$ is obtained
$$
b=k_2 \sin ^2 27^{\circ}
$$

From Eqn. (2)
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad E_{1 \min } & =k_1^2 \sin ^4 9^{\circ} \cot 9^{\circ}+\left(k_1 \cos ^2 9\right)^2 \tan 9^{\circ} \\
& =k_1^2 \sin 9^{\circ} \cos 9^{\circ}\left(\sin ^2 9^{\circ}+\cos ^2 9^{\circ}\right) \\
& =k_1^2 \sin 9^{\circ} \cos 9^{\circ}
\end{aligned}
$$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Similarly } E_2 \min =k_2^2 \sin 27^{\circ} \cos 27^{\circ} \\ & \therefore \quad E_{\text {min }}=E_{1 \text { min }}+E_{2 \text { min }} \\ & k_1^2 \sin 9^{\circ} \cos 9^{\circ}+k_2^2 \sin 27^{\circ} \cos 27^{\circ} \\ & =\frac{1}{2}\left[k_1^2 \sin 18^{\circ}+\left(5-k_1\right)^2 \sin 54^{\circ}\right] \\ & =\frac{1}{2}\left[k_1^2\left(\sin 18^{\circ}+\cos 36^{\circ}\right) 10 k_1 \cos 36^{\circ}+25 \cos 36^{\circ}\right] \\ & E_{\min }=\frac{-D}{4 A} \text { (quadratic in } k_1 \text { ) } \\ & =+\frac{25}{2}\left[\frac{\cos 36^{\circ} \sin 18^{\circ}}{\sin 18^{\circ}+\cos 36^{\circ}}\right] \\ & =\frac{25}{4 \sqrt{5}}=\frac{5 \sqrt{5}}{4}=\frac{\sqrt{125}}{4}=\frac{\sqrt{x}}{4} \\ & x+y=129 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Given that $x \in R$, find the minimum value of $(3 \sqrt{5-4 \cos x}+\sqrt{13-12 \sin x})^2$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} E & =(3 \sqrt{5-4 \cos x}+\sqrt{13-12 \sin x}) \\ & =(\sqrt{45-36 \cos x}+\sqrt{13-12 \sin x}) \\ & =\left(\sqrt{(6-3 \cos x)^2+(3 \sin x)^2}+\sqrt{(2-3 \sin x)^2+(3 \cos x)^2}\right) \\ & =P A+\left.P B\right|_{\text {minimum }}=A B=\sqrt{40}\end{aligned}$
Where
$$
\begin{aligned}
& P=3 \cos x, 3 \sin x \\
& A=(6,0) \text { and } B=(0,2)
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, minimum value of $E^2=40$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=\left(\frac{a^{\sin ^4 x}}{a^{2 \sin ^2 x}}\right)^3 \cdot\left(a^{5 \sin ^3 x} \cdot a^{5 \sin x}\right)$ and $b=(\sqrt{5})^{2 \log _5 3}(a\gt0, a \neq 1)$. If the minimum value of $\left(f(x) \cdot a^b\right)^{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}+\log _a \sin x$ is equal to $a^k, k \in N$, find $k$.
questions.solution:
$\because \quad f(x)=a^{3 \sin ^4 x+5 \sin ^3 x-6 \sin ^2 x+5 \sin x} \quad$ and $b=3 \quad(\sin x\gt0)$
$\therefore \quad\left(f(x) \cdot a^3\right)^{\operatorname{cosec}^2 x}=a^{3 \sin ^2 x+5 \sin x-6+\frac{5}{\sin x}+\frac{3}{\sin ^2 x}}$
$=\mathrm{a}^{3\left(\sin ^2 x+\frac{1}{\sin ^2 x}\right)+5\left(\sin x+\frac{1}{\sin x}\right)-6}$
$\because \quad$ min. value of $3\left(\sin ^2 x+\frac{1}{\sin ^2 x}\right)+5\left(\sin x+\frac{1}{\sin x}\right)-6 \quad($ for $\sin x\gt0)$
$=3(2)+5(2)-6=10$
( $\because \sin \mathrm{x}\gt0$ and the value is minimum at $\sin \mathrm{x}=1$ )
$\Rightarrow \log _a(\sin x)=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the sum of the roots of the equation $\cos 4 x+6=7 \cos 2 x$ in the interval $[0,314]$ is $k \pi, k \in R$. Find k .
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \cos 4 x+6=7 \cos 2 x \\ & 2 \cos ^2 2 x-1+6=7 \cos 2 x \\ & 2 \cos ^2 2 x-7 \cos 2 x+5=0 \\ & 2 \cos ^2 2 x-2 \cos 2 x-5 \cos 2 x+5=0 \\ & 2 \cos 2 x(\cos 2 x-1)-5(\cos 2 x-1)=0 \\ & (\cos 2 x-1)(2 \cos 2 x-5)=0\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \cos 2 x=\frac{5}{2} \quad(\text { not possible }) \\ & \cos 2 x=1\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & 2 x=2 n \pi \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x=n \pi\end{aligned}$
sum in $[0,314]$ is
$\pi[1+2+3+\ldots \ldots+100]-100 \pi=5050 \pi-100 \pi=4950 \pi \Rightarrow k=4950$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Given that for $a, b, c, d \in R$, if $a \sec \left(200^{\circ}\right)-c \tan \left(200^{\circ}\right)=d$ and $b \sec \left(200^{\circ}\right)+d \tan \left(200^{\circ}\right)=c$ then $\frac{a^2+b^2+c^2+d^2}{a c-b d}=p \operatorname{cosec}\left(200^{\circ}\right)$ find the value of $p$ $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{a}{\cos \left(200^{\circ}\right)}-\frac{c \sin \left(200^{\circ}\right)}{\cos \left(200^{\circ}\right)}=d ....(i)\\
& \frac{b}{\cos \left(200^{\circ}\right)}+\frac{d \sin \left(200^{\circ}\right)}{\cos \left(200^{\circ}\right)}=c ....(ii)\\
& a=d \cos \left(200^{\circ}\right)+c \sin \left(200^{\circ}\right) .....(iii)\\
& b=c \cos \left(200^{\circ}\right)-d \sin \left(200^{\circ}\right) ....(iv)\\
& \therefore a^2+b^2=c^2+d^2 \\
& \therefore\left(a^2+b^2+c^2+d^2\right)=2\left(c^2+d^2\right) \\
& \quad a c=d c \cos \left(200^{\circ}\right)+c^2 \sin \left(200^{\circ}\right)
\end{aligned}$
$b d=\operatorname{cd} \cos \left(200^{\circ}\right)-d^2 \sin \left(200^{\circ}\right)$
$Also, a c-b d=\left(c^2+d^2\right) \sin \left(200^{\circ}\right)$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { So } \frac{a^2+b^2+c^2+d^2}{a c-b d}=\frac{2\left(c^2+d^2\right)}{\left(c^2+d^2\right) \sin \left(200^{\circ}\right)}=2 \operatorname{cosec}\left(200^{\circ}\right) \\
& \therefore p=2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Find the number of solution of the equation $2^{\sin ^2 x}+5 \cdot 2^{\cos ^2 x}=7$ in the interval $[0,315]$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 2^{\sin ^2 x}+5 \cdot 2^{\cos ^2 x}=7 \\ & 2^{\sin ^2 x}+5 \cdot 2^{1-\sin ^2 x}=7 \\ & 2^{\sin ^2 x}=t \Rightarrow t+\frac{10}{t}=7\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow t^2-7 t+10=0 \Rightarrow(t-5)(t-2)=0 \Rightarrow t=2 \text { or } 5 \\ & \Rightarrow t=2^{\sin ^2 x}=2 \text { or } 5\end{aligned}$
but $2^{\sin ^2 x} \neq 5$
(think !)
$\therefore \quad 2^{\sin ^2 x}=2=2^1 \Rightarrow \sin ^2 x=1$
Hence values of x in $[0,315]$ is $\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{3 \pi}{2}, \frac{5 \pi}{2}, \ldots . \frac{199 \pi}{2}$
$\therefore$ total number of solution in $[0,315]$ is 100
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\alpha=\sum_{k=1}^{20} \frac{\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{3} k\right)}{4 \cos ^2\left(\frac{k \pi}{9}\right)-1}$ and $\beta=\sum_{k=1}^{20} \frac{\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{3} k\right)}{1-4 \sin ^2\left(\frac{k \pi}{9}\right)}$. If $\beta^2-\alpha^2=1+\cos \lambda^{\circ}$ then find the value of $\lambda$.
questions.solution:
$\alpha=\sin \frac{\pi}{9}+\sin \frac{2 \pi}{9}+\sin \frac{3 \pi}{9}+\ldots \ldots+\sin \frac{20 \pi}{9}=\frac{\sin \left(20 \cdot \frac{\pi}{18}\right)}{\sin \frac{\pi}{18}} \cdot \sin \left(\frac{21 \pi}{18}\right)=\cos \frac{\pi}{18}=\cos 10^{\circ}$
$$
\begin{array}{rlrl}
\text { IIhy } & \beta & =\sqrt{3} \cos \frac{\pi}{18}=\sqrt{3} \cos 10^{\circ} \\
\beta^2-\alpha^2 & =2 \cos ^2 10^{\circ}=1+\cos 20^{\circ} \equiv 1+\cos \lambda^{\circ} \\
\therefore & \lambda=20
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Find the value of $16\left(\sin ^2 18^{\circ}+\sin ^2 36^{\circ}+\sin ^2 54^{\circ}+\sin ^2 72^{\circ}\right)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \sin ^2 18^{\circ}+\sin ^2 36^{\circ}+\sin ^2 54^{\circ}+\sin ^2 72^{\circ}=\sin ^2 18^{\circ}+\sin ^2 36^{\circ}+\cos ^2 36^{\circ}+\cos ^2 18^{\circ}=2 \\ & \Rightarrow 16(2)=32\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $x=\frac{4 \sin 80^{\circ} \sin 65^{\circ} \cos 55^{\circ}}{\sin 20^{\circ}+\sin 50^{\circ}+\sin 70^{\circ}}$, then $x$ is:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Denominator }=\sin 20^{\circ}+2 \sin 60^{\circ} \cdot \cos 10^{\circ}=\sin 20^{\circ}+\sqrt{3} \cos 10^{\circ} \\ & \text { and Numerator }=2 \sin 80^{\circ}\left(\sin 120^{\circ}+\sin 10^{\circ}\right) \\ & =2 \sin 80^{\circ} \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}+\cos 70^{\circ}-\cos 90^{\circ}=\sqrt{3} \cos 10^{\circ}+\sin 20^{\circ} \\ & \therefore \quad x=1 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\left(\sin 25^{\circ}+\sqrt{3} \cos 85^{\circ}+\sin 85^{\circ}\right)^2=a+b \cos 50^{\circ}$, then $(a+b)=$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \text { LHS } & =\left(\sqrt{3} \sin 55^{\circ}+\sqrt{3} \sin 5^{\circ}\right)^2=3 \cos ^2 25^{\circ}=\frac{3}{2}\left(1+\cos 50^{\circ}\right) \\ \therefore \quad a & =b=\frac{3}{2}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\lambda=\left(\frac{\cos 65^{\circ}+\sqrt{3} \cos 85^{\circ}+\sin 85^{\circ}}{\sin 65^{\circ}}\right)^2$, then $\lambda=$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lambda=\left(\frac{\cos 65^{\circ}+\sqrt{3} \cos 85^{\circ}+\sin 85^{\circ}}{\sin 65^{\circ}}\right)^2=\left(\frac{\cos 65^{\circ}+2 \sin 145^{\circ}}{\sin 65^{\circ}}\right)^2=\left(\frac{\sin 25^{\circ}+\sin 35^{\circ}+\sin 35^{\circ}}{\sin 65^{\circ}}\right)^2 \\ & \lambda=\left(\frac{\sin 85^{\circ}+\sin 35^{\circ}}{\sin 65^{\circ}}\right)^2=\left(\frac{2 \sin 60^{\circ} \cos 25^{\circ}}{\sin 65^{\circ}}\right)^2=3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $0 < \alpha < 30^{\circ}$, then find the value of $\log _2\left(\frac{8 \cos \left(30^{\circ}-2 \alpha\right)-\frac{8}{\cot \alpha+\cot \left(30^{\circ}-\alpha\right)}}{\sqrt{3}}\right)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} E & =\log _2\left(\frac{P}{\sqrt{3}}\right) \\ \therefore \quad P & =8 \cos (30-2 \alpha)-\frac{8 \sin \alpha \cdot \sin (30-\alpha)}{\cos \alpha \sin (30-\alpha)+\cos (30-\alpha) \sin \alpha} \\ & =8 \cos (30-2 \alpha)-\frac{4\left(\cos (30-2 \alpha)-\cos 30^{\circ}\right)}{\sin 30^{\circ}} \\ & =8 \cos (30-2 \alpha)-8 \cos (30-2 \alpha)+4 \sqrt{3}=4 \sqrt{3} \\ E & =\log _2\left(\frac{P}{\sqrt{3}}\right)=\log _2 4=2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $L$ be the least value of the expression $\frac{\cot 2 x-\tan 2 x}{1+\sin \left(\frac{5 \pi}{2}-8 x\right)}$ if $x \in\left(0, \frac{\pi}{8}\right)$ and $M$ denotes the value of $\frac{4\left(\cos ^3 10^{\circ}+\sin ^3 20^{\circ}\right)}{\left(\cos 10^{\circ}+\sin 20^{\circ}\right)}$. Find the value of $(L+M)$
questions.solution:
L: Expression reduces to $2 \operatorname{cosec} 8 x$
$\Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{L}=\mathbf{2}$
M: We have
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{4\left(\cos ^3 10^{\circ}+\sin ^3 20^{\circ}\right)}{\left(\cos 10^{\circ}+\sin 20^{\circ}\right)}=\frac{4 \cos ^3 10^{\circ}+4 \sin ^3 20^{\circ}}{\cos 10^{\circ}+\sin 20^{\circ}}=\frac{\cos 30^{\circ}+3 \cos 10^{\circ}+3 \sin 20^{\circ}-\sin 60^{\circ}}{\cos 10^{\circ}+\sin 20^{\circ}} \\ & =\frac{3\left(\cos 10^{\circ}+\sin 20^{\circ}\right)}{\left(\cos 10^{\circ}+\sin 20^{\circ}\right)}=3=\mathrm{M} \text { Ans. }\end{aligned}$
Hence $L+M=2+3=5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Find the value of $32 \sin \frac{90^{\circ}}{17} \cos \frac{180^{\circ}}{17} \cos \frac{360^{\circ}}{17} \cos \frac{720^{\circ}}{17}$.
If the sum of the roots of the equation $\cos 4 x+6=7 \cos 2 x$ in the interval $[0,314]$ is $k \pi, k \in R$. Find k.
questions.solution:
Let $\theta=\left(\frac{90}{17}\right)^{\circ} \Rightarrow 17 \theta=90^{\circ}$

Now, $32 \sin \frac{90^{\circ}}{17} \cos \frac{180^{\circ}}{17} \cos \frac{360^{\circ}}{17} \cos \frac{720^{\circ}}{17}=32 \sin \theta \cos 2 \theta \cos 4 \theta \cos 8 \theta$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{32 \cos \theta \sin \theta \cos 2 \theta \cos 4 \theta \cos 8 \theta}{\cos \theta}=2 \times \frac{\sin 16 \theta}{\cos \theta} \\ & =\frac{2 \sin (17 \theta-\theta)}{\cos \theta}=\frac{2 \sin \left(90^{\circ}-\theta\right)}{\cos \theta}=2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the equation $\left|2 x+\sin ^2 a\right|+|2 x+3+2 \sin a|=0$ has exactly one solution $x=\lambda$ (where ' $a$ ' is a constant) then find the value of $4 \lambda^2$.
questions.solution: $\begin{array}{rlrlrl} & \left|2 x+\sin ^2 a\right|+|2 x+3+2 \sin a| & =0 & & \\ \Rightarrow & 2 x+\sin ^2 a & =0 & \text { and } & 2 x+3+2 \sin a & =0 \\ \text { Hence, } & \sin ^2 a-2 \sin a-3 & =0 \Rightarrow & \Rightarrow & \sin a=-1 \\ \therefore & 2 x & =-1 \Rightarrow & & & x=\lambda=-1 / 2 \\ \therefore & 4 \lambda^2 & =1 & & & \end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
